title: alyssum montanum subsp. collicola
intro: Alyssum montanum subsp. collicola, also known as mountain alyssum, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Brassicaceae, characterized by its small white or pinkish flowers and delicate foliage. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional medicine, where it is used to treat various health issues, including respiratory problems, digestive issues, and skin conditions, due to its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of alyssum montanum subsp. collicola include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and boost the immune system. In herbal preparations, the plant can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, allowing for its medicinal properties to be harnessed and used in a variety of applications.
